findings.message: Tautological assertion `assert s is s` can never fail — it asserts an object is identical to itself and tests nothing about Settings' behaviour. This test passes regardless of any change to the code under test.
findings.suggestion: Replace with a meaningful assertion about Settings, e.g. assert a known field has its expected default (`assert Settings().log_level == 'INFO'`) or remove the test entirely.

findings.message: Over-mocked: the test monkeypatches `Settings.resolved_log_level` (the very property under test) to return 'MOCKED', then asserts it equals 'MOCKED'. It only verifies that `unittest.mock.patch` works — it cannot fail regardless of the real `resolved_log_level` implementation.
findings.suggestion: Drive the real property via its inputs (e.g. set APP_LOG_LEVEL via monkeypatch.setenv or construct Settings with explicit fields) and assert the resolved value the production code computes, without patching the property itself.
